title: quercus × beturica
intro: Quercus × betulifolia, also known as Quercus × betulifolia or a hybrid of the English oak (Quercus robur) and the sessile oak (Quercus petraea), is a deciduous tree native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Quercus × betulifolia lie in its bark, which contains tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it a potential source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents. The health benefits of Quercus × betulifolia include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against infections and diseases. Quercus × betulifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, often used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
